title: Gate on fast-tier p99 and standard-tier p95
preview: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The int...
text: Summary: This PR adds tier-aware SLO gating to perf-canary for the Workload-aware Batching Defaults launch. We now fail the perf-canary run if: (1) FAST tier p99 latency regresses beyond threshold (tail-latency protection gate), or (2) STANDARD tier p95 latency regresses beyond threshold (steady-state gate). The intent is to stop merges that silently violate the published tier SLOs while still allowing legitimate improvements to land with a controlled baseline refresh process. Motivation: As we move batching config from ad-hoc per-route knobs to three customer-facing tiers (fast / standard / cost-efficient), we need CI guardrails that match what we’ll ship. Previously, canary gating was based on a single aggregate p95 and a single scenario per model. That missed fast-tier tail regressions (p99) and could overreact to noise in standard tier when concurrency ramps changed. What changed ...

title: Add auto-rollback dashboards + alert rules (triggered/suppressed/stale monitors) for rollout safety
preview: Context: Redwood rollouts now evaluate SLO signals (latency/error burn-rate + staleness) and can auto-pause/auto-rollback. We need first-class operator visibility and sane alerts before enabling this broadly in production. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for auto-rollback outcomes (trigger...
text: Context: Redwood rollouts now evaluate SLO signals (latency/error burn-rate + staleness) and can auto-pause/auto-rollback. We need first-class operator visibility and sane alerts before enabling this broadly in production. This PR adds Grafana dashboards and Prometheus alert rules for auto-rollback outcomes (triggered/paused/suppressed), monitor coverage & staleness, and a per-service rollout health view. Dashboards: (1) Rollout Safety — Overview: shows decision volume and rate, breakdown by service/route/model_version/region/tier, and top suppress reasons; includes a panel for “monitor missing/stale” to catch the most common gap from game day/postmortems. (2) Rollout Safety — Rollout Detail: scoped to rollout_id; shows decision timeline + links to rollout controller logs and SLO monitor ids (via labels). (3) Rollout Safety — Service Health: operators pick service + route; shows reque...

title: Post rollout auto-rollback events to Slack (concise notification + links)
preview: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, ...
text: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, (2) the rollout SLO dashboard, and (3) the audit event details. Motivation: during the 2026-02 simulations and recent customer escalations, oncall often learned about auto-mitigations only after secondary alerts or customer reports; this closes the loop and reduces time-to-context while keeping noise bounded. Event source: consumes the new structured audit event emitted to the event stream (redwood PR-28435) with event_type=rollout.auto_action.*. The handler maps event subtypes to Slack severity and routing without paging by default (notify-only) to avoid paging storms. Mes...

title: Add Azure Sentinel (Log Analytics) export target + Private deployment config examples
preview:
Context
We’re standardizing audit event schema v1 across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and expanding supported SIEM export targets. This PR adds an Azure Sentinel / Log Analytics sink to audit-log-exporter using the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API (DCE + DCR) and includes configuration examples for Private deployments.

text:
Context
We’re standardizing audit event schema v1 across Hosted/Dedicated/Private and expanding supported SIEM export targets. This PR adds an Azure Sentinel / Log Analytics sink to audit-log-exporter using the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API (DCE + DCR) and includes configuration examples for Private deployments.

Why
- Multiple enterprise/security reviews (and SUP-18477) requested first-class Sentinel support.
- Private customers want a supported path that doesn’t rely on “roll your own” forwarders.
- We need consistent payload validation + schema_version tagging before exporting to third-party systems (ties to ENG-4827).

What’s in this PR
1) New sink: azure_sentinel
- Adds a new output target: type=azure_sentinel
- Uses Azure AD OAuth2 client credentials to obtain an access token.
- Posts batches to Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion endpoint (DCE) for a configured Data Collection Rule...

title: Add Suggestions API + funnel Grafana dashboards and beta alerting
preview: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative ca...
text: Summary: Adds a first-pass set of Grafana dashboards + alert rules for the Optimization Suggestions beta, covering (1) Suggestions API health (latency/errors/payload) and (2) the Suggestions view funnel (impression → expand → dismiss → apply-click), including breakdowns by org/model/route/region with conservative cardinality controls. Motivation: during dogfood we had a p95 regression + early customer reports of “empty despite traffic” and “estimates look off”; we need a single place for SRE/Platform + product to validate the pipeline (compute freshness), the API (error/latency), and the UX funnel (are people seeing/using suggestions). Scope notes: This PR only adds dashboards/alerts in observability-pack; it assumes the backend/UI already emits the metrics/events (ENG-19402) and the Suggestions API exists (ENG-19341). No changes to application code here. Dashboards added: (A) Optimiz...

title: Audit log pipeline dashboards + alerts (ingestion latency, drop rate) with runbook links
preview:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the audit logging standardization effort we’re tightening operational visibility for the audit event ingestion/export path. We’ve had a couple of near-misses where ingestion lag spiked without a clear on-call landing page, and early alert drafts lacked enough context to triage quickly.

This PR adds:
- A Grafana dashboard for the audit-log ingestion pipeline (end-to-end delivery latency, ingest backlog, drop rate, retry/429/5xx, DLQ growth).
- Alert rules for delivery latency and drop rate with sane defaults + per-environment routing.
- Runbook links embedded in alert annotations and dashboard panel descriptions.

Key signals / definitions:
- delivery_latency_seconds: time from event emission at producer to ack at ingestion sink (measured via audit-ingest gateway metrics + exporter timestamps).
- drop_rate: fraction of events not successfully delivered after retry ...

title: Quality gates: dashboards + alert rules (pass rate, failure spikes, latency, flake rate)
preview: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook l...
text: Adds first-class observability for Optimize quality gate runs. This PR introduces (1) a Grafana dashboard for the full gate-run funnel (created → running → passed/failed/overridden), (2) Prometheus alert rules for gate failure spikes, flake rate regressions, and gate execution latency/SLO breaches, and (3) runbook links + consistent labels so we can slice by config_type (batching/cache/quant_profile), model_family, route_id, account_tier, and region. Motivation: we’re rolling out mandatory gating ahead of applying Optimize recommendations; we need to catch (a) real quality regressions, (b) eval-harness instability/flaky suites causing false blocks, and (c) infrastructure constraints causing timeouts (notably EU-West). Design notes: alerts avoid paging on single-run failures; they trigger on burn-rate / sustained increases and include a ‘possible flake’ annotation when rerun aggregatio...

title: Monitor adapter: query latency + error SLO burn-rates (with staleness + sample sizes)
preview: Context: Auto-rollback on SLO breach needs the rollout controller to fetch burn-rate status for the exact rollout scope (route/model_version/region/customer_tier) and make decisions with guardrails (sample size + staleness handling). This PR adds a monitor adapter in `redwood` that queries the internal slo-toolkit a...
text:
Context: Auto-rollback on SLO breach needs the rollout controller to fetch burn-rate status for the exact rollout scope (route/model_version/region/customer_tier) and make decisions with guardrails (sample size + staleness handling). This PR adds a monitor adapter in `redwood` that queries the internal slo-toolkit and normalizes results for the rollout controller.

What’s in this PR:
- New `SloMonitorAdapter` interface + `SloToolkitAdapter` implementation.
- Fetches burn-rate status for latency (p95/p99) and error-rate SLOs, returning both fast/slow windows (e.g., 5m/30m) and per-window sample sizes.
- Staleness detection:
 - Treats a monitor result as STALE when `now - data_timestamp > staleness_threshold` (default 2m; configurable).
 - Treats missing timestamps or NaN burn-rate as UNKNOWN (distinct from OK/BREACH).
- Resilience:
 - Timeouts + bounded retries when calling slo-toolkit...

title: Add SDK retry/timeout/streaming disconnect dashboard panels + rollout alerts
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the...
text:
Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity rollout (standard retries/backoff, timeout model, and streaming disconnect handling), SRE requested first-class dashboards and conservative alerts to monitor load impact and customer-facing failure rates during beta/stable releases. This PR adds (1) new panels to the existing "SDK Client Health" Grafana dashboard for retry rate, timeout rate, and streaming disconnect reasons, (2) companion alert rules with rollout-friendly thresholds and burn-rate style paging, and (3) a small set of recording rules to make the queries cheaper/consistent across environments.

What’s included: - Dashboard panels: Retry rate (by sdk_language, endpoint, model, status_code), Retry attempts distribution (p50/p95 of retry_count per request), Timeout rate (by sdk_language, timeout_scope=overall|connect|read), Timeout duration (histogram heatmap where availabl...

title: Add Quality Gate enforcement check stage to perf-canary pipeline
preview: Background: Optimize now requires quality gates before applying cost-saving config diffs (batching/cache/quantization). We’ve had a near-miss during beta where enforcement drifted between services (Optimize apply endpoint blocked correctly, rollout promotion didn’t). This PR adds a dedicated perf-canary stage that c...
text:
Background: Optimize now requires quality gates before applying cost-saving config diffs (batching/cache/quantization). We’ve had a near-miss during beta where enforcement drifted between services (Optimize apply endpoint blocked correctly, rollout promotion didn’t). This PR adds a dedicated perf-canary stage that continuously verifies gate enforcement semantics end-to-end against a controlled staging route, and alerts on regressions.

What this adds:
- New canary stage: `quality_gate_enforcement`
 - Executes an end-to-end scenario against staging Optimize + eval-harness + rollout-controller API surface.
 - Validates enforcement at the apply boundary (blocked when gate != PASSED) and at promotion boundary (canary promotion blocked on failed gate).
- A small, deterministic test fixture:
 - Uses a dedicated canary account + route (non-production) and a synthetic config diff that is expe...

title: Fix rate-limit/quota header consistency across endpoints + add regression tests
preview: Context: Support has multiple reports of confusing/incorrect rate-limit headers after SDK upgrades and during fallback routing (SUP-1843, SUP-1855). In particular: (1) some endpoints emitted RateLimit-* while others only emitted X-RateLimit-*; (2) reset values were inconsistently rounded (ms vs s); (3) quota-limit v...
text:
Context: Support has multiple reports of confusing/incorrect rate-limit headers after SDK upgrades and during fallback routing (SUP-1843, SUP-1855). In particular: (1) some endpoints emitted RateLimit-* while others only emitted X-RateLimit-*; (2) reset values were inconsistently rounded (ms vs s); (3) quota-limit vs request-rate-limit were conflated, resulting in 429 responses with headers that suggested a long-term quota exhaustion when the user was simply bursting; (4) in a subset of fallback codepaths, we emitted headers computed from the upstream pool defaults instead of the customer/account plan, causing Remaining to jump between requests. This PR standardizes header emission across all public inference endpoints, splits "rate limit" vs "quota" semantics, and adds regression coverage for gateway + fallback paths.

What’s included: - Header standardization: Always emit IETF-style...

title: Add audit log lag dashboard + alerts and wire runbook links (ENG-4824)
preview: Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensur...
text:
Context: Support + CS escalations (notably around audit log delivery latency) highlighted that we lacked a first-class, Support/CS-friendly view of end-to-end audit log pipeline lag and an alert that aligns to enterprise expectations. This PR adds a dedicated dashboard and alerting rules for audit log lag, and ensures panels/alerts link to a canonical runbook. It is intended to reduce time-to-triage during tickets like SUP-1846 and to support the known-issues sprint playbook work by providing an authoritative signal.

What’s included: (1) New Grafana dashboard: "Audit Logs — Delivery Lag" with panels for ingestion → transform → delivery stages, p50/p95/p99 lag, backlog depth (where available), per-region breakdown, and a quick "is this customer-impacting" section (current lag vs expected SLO). (2) New Prometheus alerts: audit-log-lag warning + critical, scoped to production namespaces...

text:
Title: Make quality gating policy configurable per-route and account tier

Context
Today, quality gates are effectively “one size fits all” once the feature flag is enabled. This creates friction for:
- Self-serve routes where we want a fast smoke gate by default (DX) and escalated full gates for higher-risk diffs.
- Enterprise / regulated routes where we want stricter defaults (full gates always, tighter override rules) and explicit auditability.

This PR introduces a first-class gating policy object that can be resolved by (route_id, account_tier, model_family) with sane defaults. Enforcement remains defense-in-depth (Optimize apply boundary + rollout promotion), but now reads a consistent policy source.

What’s in this PR
1) Policy model + resolution
- Adds GatePolicy and GatePolicyBinding data model.
- Resolution order:
 1. Route override (route_id)
 2. Tier default (account_tier)...

title: Prompt for + pin related SUP tickets and customer-impact summary in incident bridge channels
preview: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge cre...
text: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge creator for related SUP tickets and a customer-impact summary, (2) post and pin a single "Escalation context" message in the bridge channel, and (3) keep the channel topic synced with SUP links (without including customer names). Behavior: - When a bridge channel is created via the bot (or when the bot detects it has been invited to a newly created bridge channel), the bot posts a message with two required actions: "Add related Jira tickets" (accepts SUP-* and optionally INT-*), and "Add customer impact" (free text w/ guidance). - Once submitted, the bot edits the message into...

title: Add representative workload suite to nightly canary + gating mode for release branches
preview: Context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” project, we need the representative suite to run continuously (nightly) and also to be usable as a release gate for runtime release branches. This PR wires the representative suite runner into perf-canary with two execution modes: (1) ni...
text: Context: As part of the Q1 2026 “Perf baseline & representative workload suite” project, we need the representative suite to run continuously (nightly) and also to be usable as a release gate for runtime release branches. This PR wires the representative suite runner into perf-canary with two execution modes: (1) nightly mode (signal + trend) and (2) gating mode (pass/fail) intended for release/* branches. Summary of changes: - Adds a new job definition for the representative suite (chat streaming, long-context prefill, embeddings high-QPS; tool-calling is stubbed in config but not enabled by default yet). - Introduces a `mode` flag: `nightly` vs `gating`. Nightly publishes full metric payloads and trends; gating evaluates a smaller set of canonical metrics against threshold config and emits a single PASS/FAIL with links to artifacts. - Adds threshold configuration files keyed by (mod...

title: Improve streaming disconnect errors (request ID propagation + clearer classification)
preview: Context: Support escalations around streaming resets/truncation (notably us-east) have been slowed by ambiguous client-visible errors and missing request IDs in some disconnect paths. This PR improves server-side error classification for streaming disconnects and ensures request ID propagation is consistent across g...
text: Context: Support escalations around streaming resets/truncation (notably us-east) have been slowed by ambiguous client-visible errors and missing request IDs in some disconnect paths. This PR improves server-side error classification for streaming disconnects and ensures request ID propagation is consistent across gateway -> runtime -> client responses/logs. The goal is to let Support quickly distinguish (a) client/proxy disconnects, (b) upstream runtime cancellations, and (c) internal gateway timeouts, and to reliably hand customers a request_id they can paste back into tickets. Changes: (1) Introduce a dedicated StreamingDisconnect error category with stable public error codes and safe-to-share messaging. (2) Guarantee request_id inclusion in error payloads for streaming responses (including mid-stream termination) and in trailing metadata where applicable. (3) Improve logging: emit...

title: perf-canary: add paging (Sev2/Sev1) alert rules for sustained per-model regressions
preview: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Mot...
text: Summary: This adds paging-tier perf-canary alert rules (Sev2 + Sev1) for sustained regressions on top models. These are intentionally stricter than the warning rules in pr-19179 (higher minimum sample size, longer sustain window, and stricter anti-flap) and route to the on-call rotation channel via incident-bot. Motivation: During phase-1 rollout we saw warning-level regressions that were actionable when they sustained (real tail-latency impact), but the existing rules were designed to be noisy-tolerant and non-paging. This PR implements the paging path that SRE approved in the paging policy thread and is required for rollout gates integration. Alerting model: We page only for top-model + sustained regressions where we have enough canary volume to reduce false positives, and we gate on a canary-health signal (telemetry freshness + request loop healthy) to avoid paging on broken canary...

title: Add Shadow Traffic quality delta dashboards + scoring lag panels (cardinality-safe)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw r...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a first-cut set of Grafana dashboards to visualize Shadow Traffic quality deltas (control vs shadow variants) and scoring pipeline lag/health. The dashboards are designed for Hosted/Dedicated/Private and intentionally avoid high-cardinality breakdowns (no prompt_id, request_fingerprint, raw route_id, customer_id).

This is intended to support beta oncall readiness and help triage the two main failure classes seen during dogfood/beta:
1) quality deltas missing/stale due to scoring lag or ingestion lag
2) metrics backend pressure due to accidental high-cardinality dimensions

## What’s included
- New "Shadow Traffic / Quality Deltas" dashboard:
 - Δ quality score (shadow - control) over time with confidence bands (when emitted)
 - refusal rate delta, format adherence delta
 - sample size panels (n_control, n_shadow) + effective sample rate
 - breakdowns limited t...

title: Fix apply/rollback race condition causing mixed worker optimization states
preview: Context: During the rollback gameday + a customer escalation (SUP-18503), we reproduced a race where an operator-triggered rollback could overlap with an in-flight apply stage. In certain interleavings, some workers would commit the new optimization config while others would revert to the previous version, leaving t...
text:
Context: During the rollback gameday + a customer escalation (SUP-18503), we reproduced a race where an operator-triggered rollback could overlap with an in-flight apply stage. In certain interleavings, some workers would commit the new optimization config while others would revert to the previous version, leaving the fleet in a mixed state until the next periodic reconcile. This PR makes the runtime apply pipeline linearizable per (tenant, model, region) scope by introducing an apply-epoch token and CAS-style commit semantics at the worker checkpoint boundary.

What changes: (1) Control plane now includes a monotonic `apply_epoch` in the runtime apply intent payload; apply and rollback both bump epoch. (2) Workers persist the last committed (config_version, apply_epoch) and will only commit intents with epoch >= current; older intents are treated as no-ops and still ACKed for idempot...

title: Console: Shadow experiment results page (quality deltas + example drilldowns) behind feature flag
preview:
## Summary
Adds the Console UI for viewing Shadow Experiment results: quality deltas vs control, confidence intervals, segment breakdowns, and a drilldown drawer for scored examples.

This is explicitly *read-only* UI for the beta. Creation flow remains in the existing experiment wizard (DES-611 work), and the resul...
text:
## Summary
Adds the Console UI for viewing Shadow Experiment results: quality deltas vs control, confidence intervals, segment breakdowns, and a drilldown drawer for scored examples.

This is explicitly *read-only* UI for the beta. Creation flow remains in the existing experiment wizard (DES-611 work), and the results page is linked from Rollouts timeline + the Shadow Experiments list.

Everything in this PR is gated behind `shadow_experiments_results_ui` (per tenant). When disabled, routes fall back to the existing placeholder/"Coming soon" page.

## Why
We have shadow traffic shipping in dogfood and early Dedicated beta, but teams can’t validate quality changes without an at-a-glance view of deltas and a way to inspect examples. This PR wires up the results UX to the new aggregates + examples endpoints from the Console backend (PR #24933) and renders:
- Quality delta charts (candida...

title: Add Splunk HEC export target (batching, retry/backoff, delivery metrics)
preview:
## Summary
Adds a first-class Splunk HEC export target to audit-log-exporter. The target supports (1) bounded batching to control request volume and Splunk HEC payload size, (2) retry w/ exponential backoff + jitter for transient failures, and (3) explicit delivery visibility via metrics and structured logs.

This i...
text:
## Summary
Adds a first-class Splunk HEC export target to audit-log-exporter. The target supports (1) bounded batching to control request volume and Splunk HEC payload size, (2) retry w/ exponential backoff + jitter for transient failures, and (3) explicit delivery visibility via metrics and structured logs.

This is part of the audit logging standardization effort to ensure customers can reliably export normalized audit events into common SIEMs. Splunk HEC is a frequent enterprise requirement (see SUP-18477) and has been a blocker for at least two Private-deployment evaluations.

## Motivation / context
- Customers want to ingest audit logs into Splunk with predictable field semantics and stable delivery.
- Our current exporter supports HTTP generic sinks, but Splunk HEC has specific behavior and recommended payload structure (event wrapping, source/sourcetype, token auth, and status...

title: Add schema_version tagging, JSON Schema validation, and legacy event normalization to v1
preview:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into t...
text:
## Summary
This PR updates the audit-log-exporter to:
- Tag all outgoing events with `schema_version` (default: `v1.0`).
- Validate outgoing payloads against the canonical Audit Log Schema v1 (JSON Schema) and emit exporter-side metrics when validation fails.
- Normalize legacy/heterogeneous producer payloads into the v1 canonical shape to eliminate field drift across event types.

This is the first step to making SIEM exports consistent across Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments and to support SOC2/ISO evidence expectations (stable schema + documented backcompat behavior).

## Motivation
We have multiple event producers emitting “almost the same” semantics but with different field names and shapes. Customers exporting to Splunk/Sentinel/Datadog see inconsistent keys across event types (SUP-18477). Additionally, exporter retries can cause duplicates (SUP-18510); while idempotency is ...

title: Retry-After parsing (delta-seconds + HTTP-date) + jittered exponential backoff
preview: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (retry/timeout standard), TS currently only parses Retry-After when it is a delta-seconds value. The gateway and some upstreams also emit Retry-After as an HTTP-date (e.g. "Wed, 21 Oct 2015 07:28:00 GMT"), which we should respect to avoid retrying too aggr...
text: Context: As part of the cross-language SDK parity initiative (retry/timeout standard), TS currently only parses Retry-After when it is a delta-seconds value. The gateway and some upstreams also emit Retry-After as an HTTP-date (e.g. "Wed, 21 Oct 2015 07:28:00 GMT"), which we should respect to avoid retrying too aggressively during throttling. In addition, our retry middleware used a deterministic exponential backoff sequence, which can create synchronized retries (thundering herd) across many clients. This PR adds: (1) Retry-After parsing for both delta-seconds and HTTP-date formats, (2) jittered exponential backoff consistent with the parity standard, and (3) tests/fixtures to lock behavior. Behavior details: Retry-After parsing: If header is delta-seconds => wait = clamp(deltaSeconds*1000, 0..maxRetryAfterMs). If header is HTTP-date => wait = clamp(dateMs - nowMs, 0..maxRetryAfterMs...

title: Shadow traffic: telemetry events + ingestion hooks (v1)
preview: Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingest...
text:
Context: Shadow traffic routing (ENG-5402) mirrors a sampled subset of prod requests to one or more shadow targets. To compute quality deltas (and to debug routing/scoring lag) we need a low-cardinality, privacy-aware set of telemetry events that (a) links the control and shadow inference outcomes, (b) can be ingested reliably in Hosted/Dedicated/Private, and (c) provides enough dimensions to slice by route/model/region without exploding metrics cardinality.

This PR introduces shadow traffic telemetry events and wires them into the ingestion pipeline (Kafka -> ingest -> warehouse tables) with initial dashboard stubs for internal dogfood.

What’s included: (1) New event schemas: shadow_pair_created (emitted when the mirror is scheduled), shadow_inference_completed (shadow target returned or timed out), and shadow_scoring_status (async scoring queued/completed/failed). Each event inclu...

title: Perf baseline dashboards + alert rules for representative suite (TTFT/TPOT/throughput/variance) with manifest links
preview: Context: As part of the Q1'26 perf baseline + representative workload suite, we need a standard set of dashboards and alerting that (a) make baseline runs explorable, (b) make regressions actionable, and (c) are attributable via a first-class link from the panel to the run manifest + trace artifacts. This PR adds a ...
text: Context: As part of the Q1'26 perf baseline + representative workload suite, we need a standard set of dashboards and alerting that (a) make baseline runs explorable, (b) make regressions actionable, and (c) are attributable via a first-class link from the panel to the run manifest + trace artifacts. This PR adds a versioned dashboard bundle and alert rules aimed at baseline runs + perf-canary outputs. What’s included: 1) Grafana dashboards (folder: "Perf Baselines") with panels for TTFT/TPOT percentiles, throughput, saturation proxies, and variance/noise views. 2) Alert rules for baseline regressions (non-paging by default) and canary gating signals (paging for release-branch gating only) with routing to the right channels/oncall. 3) Standard run label/templating conventions aligned with ADR-0172 (model, model_family, workload_id, region, sku, runtime_git_sha, scheduler_profile, kern...
links: Linear ENG-4828 (perf-canary integration and regression thresholds)
ADR-0172 (benchmark metrics and run manifest format)

title: runtime/scheduler: enforce KV compaction budget at decode boundaries to avoid stalls
preview: Context: This PR integrates KV-cache compaction budgeting into the scheduler loop to prevent decode-time stalls and tail-latency spikes when compaction/relocation work is triggered under load. It is part of the KV cache compaction improvements epic and builds on the allocator primitives + metrics added in earlier PR...
text:
Context: This PR integrates KV-cache compaction budgeting into the scheduler loop to prevent decode-time stalls and tail-latency spikes when compaction/relocation work is triggered under load. It is part of the KV cache compaction improvements epic and builds on the allocator primitives + metrics added in earlier PRs (notably pr-19357 and pr-19342).

Problem: Prior compaction prototype ran relocation work opportunistically when fragmentation crossed a threshold. Under mixed-length workloads, the relocation work could happen during decode hot-path and cause intermittent stalls (visible as p99 spikes and occasional perf-canary failures). We need explicit guardrails so compaction work is budgeted and scheduled only in safe windows.

What changed:
- Introduced scheduler-level compaction budget enforcement with two budgets: time (microseconds) and bytes-moved per scheduling tick.
- Enforce...

title: runtime: tier-aware LRU eviction policy for KV cache (pinned sessions + tests)
preview: Summary: Implements tier-aware LRU eviction for the serving runtime KV cache to make allocator behavior predictable under memory pressure and to reduce burst-driven tail latency caused by opportunistic/late evictions. The policy prefers evicting low-priority tiers first (e.g., free/trial) while preserving higher tie...
text:
Summary: Implements tier-aware LRU eviction for the serving runtime KV cache to make allocator behavior predictable under memory pressure and to reduce burst-driven tail latency caused by opportunistic/late evictions. The policy prefers evicting low-priority tiers first (e.g., free/trial) while preserving higher tiers (hosted paid / dedicated) and always respecting pinned sessions. This PR includes correctness-focused unit + integration tests and adds basic observability counters needed for rollout guardrails.

Motivation: During high churn with mixed sequence lengths, the KV arena frequently hits soft pressure and the existing eviction path behaves effectively FIFO-ish (depends on free-list ordering), which can evict recently-active sequences and trigger retries / cancellations at bad times. We also had a support report (SUP-28433) where pinned long-lived sessions were disproportiona...

title: Add mixed-length burst scenario + thresholds for KV compaction regressions
preview: Motivation: The KV cache compaction work (runtime-1.22) needs a perf-canary signal that reliably reproduces fragmentation + churn patterns we see in production, and that fails fast when compaction/eviction introduces decode stalls or tail latency spikes. Existing canary scenarios (steady decode, long prompts) don’t ...
text:
Motivation: The KV cache compaction work (runtime-1.22) needs a perf-canary signal that reliably reproduces fragmentation + churn patterns we see in production, and that fails fast when compaction/eviction introduces decode stalls or tail latency spikes. Existing canary scenarios (steady decode, long prompts) don’t create enough allocator churn to surface the regressions we saw in staging.

This PR adds a new scenario (`mixed_length_burst`) that generates mixed prompt lengths + bursty arrivals + cancellations, and wires pass/fail thresholds + alert routing that are specific to compaction regressions.

What’s included: - New canary scenario: mixed-length burst (configurable length distribution + burst profile) - KPI collection updates: capture p95/p99 latency, TTFT, throughput, and the runtime-exposed compaction/eviction signals when present (bytes moved / compaction time / eviction ra...

title: Runtime: KV compaction kill-switch + config plumbing (oncall-safe disable path)
preview: Context: KV cache compaction/eviction is rolling out behind flags. During canaries we need a deterministic, oncall-friendly way to disable compaction immediately (or gate it to a safe minimum) without requiring a full runtime restart, while preserving allocator invariants and avoiding relocation-in-progress hazards....
text:
Context: KV cache compaction/eviction is rolling out behind flags. During canaries we need a deterministic, oncall-friendly way to disable compaction immediately (or gate it to a safe minimum) without requiring a full runtime restart, while preserving allocator invariants and avoiding relocation-in-progress hazards.

This PR adds the config/feature-flag plumbing and a safe-disable path (“kill switch”) for KV compaction + related relocation behavior. It also wires metrics/tracing so oncall can validate that the disable took effect, and links to the runtime runbook + rollout guardrails.

What changed: (1) Introduced runtime config keys under `kv.compaction.*` with sane defaults for release/runtime-1.22: `kv.compaction.enabled` (bool), `kv.compaction.kill_switch` (bool, higher priority than enabled), `kv.compaction.budget_ms_per_tick` (int), `kv.compaction.budget_bytes_per_tick` (int), `...

title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels
preview:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while c...
text:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while context switching across tabs. This PR adds a tiny “fast path” in incident-bot: a slash command that posts the correct links and a short checklist directly into the incident channel.

What’s included
- New Slack slash command: `/rollback` (alias `/rw-rollback`).
- Posts a message with:
 - Link to Console Rollback UI (Optimization Config history view with the rollback modal entry point).
 - Link to the runbook: “Rollback Optimization Config”.
 - A reminder of the expected decision criteria (p95/p99 regression + 5xx) and the post-rollback verification checklist.
- Optional arg...

title: perf-canary: add QPS rate limits + per-region capacity budget (GPU overhead guardrail)
preview: Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts ...
text:
Context: As we move perf-canary from staging into a production region, we need hard guardrails so the canary cannot consume meaningful GPU capacity or accidentally fan out due to config mistakes. This PR implements (1) explicit QPS caps with a token-bucket limiter, and (2) a per-region capacity budget that converts an overhead target (e.g., 0.5% of region GPU capacity) into an allowed request rate/concurrency for the canary. The goal is: safe by default, predictable overhead, and an immediate kill-switch when budgets are exceeded.

What this adds (high-level): - Rate limiting: token bucket limiter at the request scheduler layer (covers both steady-state QPS and burst). - Capacity budget: `capacity_budget` config per region (defaults to 0.003 = 0.3% until we tune) with optional overrides per tier/model group. - Budget accounting: estimates GPU-seconds/request using (a) last known basel...

title: Fix deadlock in quality gate run cancelation + add regression test
preview: Context: During internal beta for Optimize quality gates, we hit a rare deadlock when a gate run is canceled while the eval-harness callback is concurrently finalizing results. This surfaced as gate runs stuck in RUNNING forever (no terminal state), blocking apply/rollout, and caused a near-miss where a canary pipel...
text: Context: During internal beta for Optimize quality gates, we hit a rare deadlock when a gate run is canceled while the eval-harness callback is concurrently finalizing results. This surfaced as gate runs stuck in RUNNING forever (no terminal state), blocking apply/rollout, and caused a near-miss where a canary pipeline sat waiting until manual intervention. Root cause is a lock ordering inversion between (a) GateRun state transitions in Optimize (DB row lock + in-memory mutex keyed by gate_run_id) and (b) cancelation handler that attempted to revoke the eval job and update state while holding the per-run mutex, while the callback path held the DB transaction and attempted to acquire the same mutex to emit completion events. Fix: remove the cross-path mutex dependency by (1) making cancelation idempotent and purely DB-driven, using a single conditional UPDATE that only transitions RUNN...

title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export
preview:
Title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export

Context
We’ve had a few regressions during quant profile experiments where we needed to confirm that a rollback actually restores baseline behavior (both performance and quality) before unpausing a rollout. This PR adds first-cl...
text:
Title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export

Context
We’ve had a few regressions during quant profile experiments where we needed to confirm that a rollback actually restores baseline behavior (both performance and quality) before unpausing a rollout. This PR adds first-class benchmark scenarios to benchmark-lab that (1) compare two quant profile versions head-to-head and (2) validate that a rollback from candidate -> baseline produces results within defined tolerances.

What’s in this PR
- New benchmark suite: quant_profile_rollback
 - Runs A/B comparisons for two quant profile versions (e.g., qp_v12 vs qp_v11) on the same prompt distribution and concurrency.
 - Runs an explicit rollback scenario: baseline -> candidate -> baseline, ensuring the second baseline run matches the first baseline run (within noise thresholds).
- Metrics captured
...

title: Optimize quality gates: add override flow w/ expiration + audit events (admin-only)
preview:
## Summary
Implements the time-bound override workflow for Optimize quality gate failures. This enables an authorized approver to temporarily override a failed gate (or a blocked apply) with an explicit reason, expiration, and an auditable trail.

Key properties:
- Overrides are **time-bound** (required `expires_at`...
text:
## Summary
Implements the time-bound override workflow for Optimize quality gate failures. This enables an authorized approver to temporarily override a failed gate (or a blocked apply) with an explicit reason, expiration, and an auditable trail.

Key properties:
- Overrides are **time-bound** (required `expires_at`) with max TTL enforcement.
- Only users with an **admin override role** may grant/extend/revoke overrides.
- Overrides are **scoped** (route + model + proposed config diff hash) to avoid blanket bypass.
- All override actions emit **audit events** (request/grant/deny/revoke/expire) and are queryable.
- Optimize apply and rollout promotion paths treat an active override as an explicit exception (still logged).

This PR is part of the “Quality guardrails gating for Optimize” project and lands the last missing piece needed for limited beta (see ENG-3927 / PM-482).

## Motivat...

title: Add gating metrics emission and tracing
preview: This PR instruments the Optimize quality-gate execution path with (1) low-cardinality metrics for funnel + latency + retry/timeout visibility, (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans across Optimize -> eval-harness -> persistence -> policy enforcement, and (3) structured logs to make it possible to debug why a gate run fail...
text: This PR instruments the Optimize quality-gate execution path with (1) low-cardinality metrics for funnel + latency + retry/timeout visibility, (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans across Optimize -> eval-harness -> persistence -> policy enforcement, and (3) structured logs to make it possible to debug why a gate run failed/was retried/was blocked without needing to scrape application logs. Motivation: during the internal beta we’ve had multiple “it failed but we don’t know where” cases (eval-harness timeouts vs persistence retries vs policy-block) and we need consistent signals before we expand to limited production rollout. Scope: metrics, traces, and logs for gate runs, plus guardrails around tag cardinality and PII. Non-goals: dashboards/alerts (tracked in observability-pack PR) and any changes to gating pass/fail logic. Implementation details: Adds a top-level span `quality_gate.run` w...

title: Console: add rollback trigger endpoint with server-side confirmation checks
preview:
Adds the Console backend endpoint used by the new “Rollback” button for Optimization Config versions. This PR focuses on server-side confirmation/guardrails so the Console UI can remain thin and we avoid dangerous client-only checks.

Key behavior
- New endpoint: POST /internal/console/optimization-config/rollbacks
...
text:
Adds the Console backend endpoint used by the new “Rollback” button for Optimization Config versions. This PR focuses on server-side confirmation/guardrails so the Console UI can remain thin and we avoid dangerous client-only checks.

Key behavior
- New endpoint: POST /internal/console/optimization-config/rollbacks
 - Triggers a rollback for a given scope (tenant + model + region) to a target config_version_id.
 - Returns a rollback_operation_id and the initial operation state.
- Confirmation model
 - Requires a confirmation payload computed server-side and echoed back by the client (type-to-confirm UX can be driven from this).
 - The server recomputes the confirmation on request and rejects if it doesn’t match (prevents stale modal confirmations).
- Guardrails (server-enforced)
 - Blast-radius summary + limits: rejects requests that exceed allowed scope cardinality for Console-trigge...

title: Add fast smoke-gates tier for batching/cache Optimize changes
preview: Context: We’re adding mandatory quality guardrails before Optimize can apply cost-saving config changes (batching policy, prefix/KV caching). Full gate runs are comprehensive but can take 30–60 min and are expensive; teams need a faster precheck to catch obvious regressions and provide tight feedback loops during it...
text: Context: We’re adding mandatory quality guardrails before Optimize can apply cost-saving config changes (batching policy, prefix/KV caching). Full gate runs are comprehensive but can take 30–60 min and are expensive; teams need a faster precheck to catch obvious regressions and provide tight feedback loops during iteration. This PR introduces a new “smoke gates” tier that runs a small, high-signal prompt subset before scheduling full gates. Scope: smoke gates are enabled only for batching + caching config diffs (NOT quantization profile changes) and only in hosted/staging initially. Summary of changes: (1) Gate tier model: Introduces GateTier=SMOKE|FULL and stores tier on GateRun. A single Optimize recommendation/apply attempt now creates a GateRunGroup with an ordered plan (SMOKE -> FULL). (2) Prompt subset selection: Adds a new eval-harness request mode specifying prompt_set_id plus...

title: Add log redaction + no-PII assertions for perf-canary request/telemetry path
preview: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped thro...
text: Motivation: perf-canary must never emit or persist customer data. While the canary uses synthetic prompts, we still had a few sharp edges where (a) debug logging could print request bodies on errors and (b) some error/reporting paths would include raw prompt text / user-provided identifiers if they ever slipped through config. This PR adds explicit redaction + hard assertions so we fail-closed and keep logs/metrics safe. Summary of changes: (1) Introduce a centralized redaction helper for log fields and error payloads; (2) Add runtime assertions that enforce "synthetic-only" prompt sources (no external prompt injection), with an opt-out only for local dev; (3) Ensure we never log request bodies, prompt strings, or headers that may contain identifiers; (4) Add "safe fields" allowlist for alert webhook payloads so incident-bot never receives prompt content; (5) Add tests that simulate e...

title: Add internal perf-canary auth scope + restricted service account for inference reads
preview: Context: The perf-canary service (see perf-canary repo PRs) needs to call internal inference endpoints continuously from prod. We do not want to reuse broad internal scopes (or customer-style API keys) because (a) blast radius is too large, (b) auditing is noisy, and (c) it complicates incident response when we need...
text:
Context: The perf-canary service (see perf-canary repo PRs) needs to call internal inference endpoints continuously from prod. We do not want to reuse broad internal scopes (or customer-style API keys) because (a) blast radius is too large, (b) auditing is noisy, and (c) it complicates incident response when we need to quickly disable only canary traffic. This PR introduces a restricted auth scope + service account intended solely for synthetic canary inference reads.

What’s in this PR:
- New auth scope: `inference.canary:read` (internal-only). This scope can only be minted for service accounts (not user tokens) and is denied in external auth flows.
- New service account type + default policy bundle: `svc-perf-canary` (control-plane managed). The policy allows:
 - POST `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/completions` against internal inference gateways
 - streaming responses
 - read-only ac...

title: Embeddings: add high-QPS mode + throughput-first report + percentile latency export
preview: Summary: Adds an embeddings workload mode designed for high-QPS / small-payload traffic shapes and a throughput-first reporting path (req/s + embeddings/s, plus tokens/s where applicable). Also exports percentile latency (p50/p95/p99) in machine-readable form for perf-canary + dashboard ingestion. This is needed for...
text:
Summary: Adds an embeddings workload mode designed for high-QPS / small-payload traffic shapes and a throughput-first reporting path (req/s + embeddings/s, plus tokens/s where applicable). Also exports percentile latency (p50/p95/p99) in machine-readable form for perf-canary + dashboard ingestion. This is needed for Q1 2026 baseline sweeps because embeddings traffic is dominated by concurrency/queueing behavior and req/s saturation, and the existing generator/reporting was text-generation-centric (TTFT/TPOT) and didn’t provide stable throughput/saturation indicators.

Motivation / context: Our representative workload suite v1 includes an embeddings cohort with production-like payload sizes and concurrency targets (high QPS, small input lengths, mostly non-streaming). For baseline comparability we need: (1) a generator that can drive stable, controlled offered load (open-loop) while st...

title: runtime: add KV allocator fragmentation counters + export hooks
preview: Context: We’ve been chasing burst-driven p95/p99 spikes that correlate with KV arena churn and suspected allocator fragmentation. Before we can tune compaction/eviction, we need allocator-level signals that are (a) cheap, (b) interpretable, and (c) consistent across Hosted/Dedicated. This PR adds a small set of frag...
text: Context: We’ve been chasing burst-driven p95/p99 spikes that correlate with KV arena churn and suspected allocator fragmentation. Before we can tune compaction/eviction, we need allocator-level signals that are (a) cheap, (b) interpretable, and (c) consistent across Hosted/Dedicated. This PR adds a small set of fragmentation/health counters/gauges emitted by the KV cache allocator, and wires them into the standard runtime metrics pipeline so SRE/Perf Canary can build guardrails and dashboards. Metrics added (names are final, definitions in code comments): kv_allocator_free_bytes (gauge): total free bytes in KV arena; kv_allocator_used_bytes (gauge): total used bytes in KV arena; kv_allocator_largest_free_block_bytes (gauge): size of largest contiguous free block (best-effort from free-list); kv_allocator_fragmentation_ratio (gauge): 1 - (largest_free_block_bytes / max(free_bytes, 1));...

title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance
preview:
Title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance

Context
We need a production-representative workload that stresses prefill (large prompt / long context) with modest decode, and we need more repeatable runs for the Q1 2026 baseline sweeps. In early dry runs we saw high variance driv...
text:
Title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance

Context
We need a production-representative workload that stresses prefill (large prompt / long context) with modest decode, and we need more repeatable runs for the Q1 2026 baseline sweeps. In early dry runs we saw high variance driven by (a) first-run JIT + memory pool ramp, (b) GPU clocks/ramp state, and (c) caching artifacts depending on prior suite order. This PR introduces (1) a long-context prefill workload generator and (2) a standardized warmup phase that is applied consistently across workloads (opt-in in config, default-on for long_context_prefill).

What’s in this PR
1) New workload: long_context_prefill
- Goal: mimic long-context summarization / doc QA traffic: large prefill, smaller decode.
- Shape:
 - prompt length bins (tokens): 8k / 16k / 32k (configurable; default mix 0.45/0.45/0.10)
 ...

title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions
preview:
Title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions

Context
Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs needs an external, repeatable “canary judge” that can detect config-induced latency regressions early and produce machine-readable gate signals. Today, on-cal...
text:
Title: perf-canary: add optimization config safeguards + gate signal publisher for latency regressions

Context
Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs needs an external, repeatable “canary judge” that can detect config-induced latency regressions early and produce machine-readable gate signals. Today, on-call relies on dashboards + manual judgement, which slows progression and increases risk (see internal incident notes around p95 spikes after caching/batching tweaks).

What this PR does
- Adds a new scheduled/on-demand canary workflow ("optimization-safeguard") that:
 - Detects active Optimization Config rollout scopes (tenant/model/region + canary %) and config_version.
 - Pulls baseline vs canary metrics (p95/p99 latency + 5xx rate) for the last N minutes, with warmup handling.
 - Evaluates against configurable thresholds with sane defaults (latency regression % + absolute ...

title: Emit structured audit log events for Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) + schema updates
preview: Context: Optimization config changes (batching/caching/quantization profiles) are production-impacting and must be traceable and reviewable. We’ve had gaps reported by enterprise customers (SUP-18544) and internal operators (INT-3390) where rollback actions were not consistently visible in audit exports, and some li...
text: Context: Optimization config changes (batching/caching/quantization profiles) are production-impacting and must be traceable and reviewable. We’ve had gaps reported by enterprise customers (SUP-18544) and internal operators (INT-3390) where rollback actions were not consistently visible in audit exports, and some lifecycle steps were only logged in app logs. This PR standardizes and emits structured audit events for the full Optimization Config lifecycle and updates the audit schema registry accordingly. What’s included: (1) New/standardized event names under the "optimization_config" namespace: optimization_config.version_created, optimization_config.version_validated, optimization_config.apply_requested, optimization_config.rollout_started, optimization_config.rollout_paused, optimization_config.rollout_resumed, optimization_config.rollout_step_completed, optimization_config.rollout...

title: Add quality gate state machine + persistence for Optimize config changes
preview: This PR introduces the Quality Gate Run state machine and persistence layer in the Optimize service. Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization profile changes) will be required to attach a gate run before they can be applied to production, but this PR only implements the core data model + transition lo...
text: This PR introduces the Quality Gate Run state machine and persistence layer in the Optimize service. Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization profile changes) will be required to attach a gate run before they can be applied to production, but this PR only implements the core data model + transition logic + initial API endpoints (wiring to eval-harness and Console happens in follow-ups). The state machine is intentionally strict and idempotent to support retries, partial failures, and eventual coupling to rollout promotion. Includes DB migrations, internal admin endpoints, and notes for backfilling existing recommendations with `gate_required=false` until policy enforcement lands.

title: runtime/scheduler: add queue time, batch assembly, and batch size distribution metrics
preview:
Title: runtime/scheduler: add queue time, batch assembly, and batch size distribution metrics

Context
Perf baseline & representative workload suite work (Q1 2026) needs better attribution for TTFT/TPOT regressions: we can currently see end-to-end latency and some GPU-level counters, but we don’t have enough schedul...
text:
Title: runtime/scheduler: add queue time, batch assembly, and batch size distribution metrics

Context
Perf baseline & representative workload suite work (Q1 2026) needs better attribution for TTFT/TPOT regressions: we can currently see end-to-end latency and some GPU-level counters, but we don’t have enough scheduler-level detail to answer: is p99 worse because requests wait longer in the queue, because batch formation takes longer, or because we’re forming suboptimal batches?

This PR adds low-overhead scheduler instrumentation for:
- Queue wait time (enqueue -> dequeued into a batch)
- Batch assembly time (start building batch -> submit to execution)
- Batch size distributions and “why” counters (e.g. flush reason)

These metrics are used by benchmark-lab/perf-suite runs and will be consumed by baseline dashboards + perf-canary gating. In production, the default configuration is co...

title: runtime: export scheduler queueing-time metrics + batch-size distribution
preview: Motivation: perf-canary is showing end-to-end latency regressions that are hard to attribute (kernel/runtime execution vs scheduler pressure). This PR exports scheduler queueing-time metrics and batch-size distribution at the runtime layer so we can: (1) split latency into queueing vs execution for canary attributio...
text: Motivation: perf-canary is showing end-to-end latency regressions that are hard to attribute (kernel/runtime execution vs scheduler pressure). This PR exports scheduler queueing-time metrics and batch-size distribution at the runtime layer so we can: (1) split latency into queueing vs execution for canary attribution, (2) detect regressions caused by batching heuristics / admission control changes, (3) avoid relying on sampling-heavy trace-only approaches. What’s included: - New histogram metric for scheduler queueing time (enqueue -> execution start). - New histogram metric for batch size at execution start (effective batch after coalescing). - Structured dimension set aligned with telemetry cardinality constraints (no per-request identifiers). - Wiring to existing runtime metrics registry + export path. - Unit tests for metric emission + bounds (no negative durations, no zero/negati...

title: Add schema + SIEM mappings for offline bundle verification audit events
preview: Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normali...
text:
Context: Redwood Private offline bundle signing/verifying is landing for air-gapped installs. The installer now emits local audit events for bundle verification outcomes (success + specific failure reasons). Customers who forward audit logs to a SIEM via audit-log-exporter need these events to be recognized, normalized, and exported in a stable schema.

What this PR does:
- Adds new event definitions to audit-log-exporter’s schema catalog for bundle verification outcomes.
- Adds normalization/mapping rules for JSON (native), CEF, and LEEF output modes.
- Ensures failure reasons are exported as a bounded enum + optional details field (redaction-aware).
- Updates unit tests + golden fixtures for all output formats.

New events (normalized):
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.succeeded
- redwood.private.bundle_verification.failed

Normalized fields:
- event.name (string)
- event.outco...

title: Implement chat streaming workload generator (request mix, concurrency ramp, token pacing) + TTFT/TPOT export
preview:
Motivation: We need a production-representative chat streaming workload for the Q1 2026 perf baseline & suite. Existing harness coverage is request/response (non-streaming) only, which under-measures scheduling + queueing dynamics and makes TTFT/TPOT comparisons noisy across client implementations.

What this adds: ...
text:
Motivation: We need a production-representative chat streaming workload for the Q1 2026 perf baseline & suite. Existing harness coverage is request/response (non-streaming) only, which under-measures scheduling + queueing dynamics and makes TTFT/TPOT comparisons noisy across client implementations.

What this adds: (1) A chat streaming workload generator (server SSE / chunked streaming) with configurable request-mix, sequence-length bins, and streaming cadence; (2) a concurrency ramp runner (warmup + stepped load) to mimic bursty traffic and reduce variance; (3) standardized baseline outputs for TTFT + TPOT with explicit measurement boundaries (client-side network excluded as much as practical).

Key behaviors: Request mix is modeled as weighted "cohorts" (e.g., short prompt + short decode, short prompt + long decode, medium prompt + medium decode) with configurable distributions. Con...

title: runtime: make optimization config applier idempotent + retry-safe (partial rollout safeguards)
preview: Context: As part of Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs, the control plane will issue apply operations that may be retried (network timeouts, controller restarts) and can be delivered out-of-order relative to worker restarts and shard movement. Prior implementation applied configs as a best-effort "set cu...
text:
Context: As part of Safe apply + rollback for Optimization Configs, the control plane will issue apply operations that may be retried (network timeouts, controller restarts) and can be delivered out-of-order relative to worker restarts and shard movement. Prior implementation applied configs as a best-effort "set current" and would re-run side effects on every delivery (cache invalidation, batcher reinit), which increased tail latency and could leave mixed worker states during partial rollout. This PR makes the runtime Optimization Config applier idempotent and resilient to retries, and adds explicit safeguards for partial rollout states.

What changed: 1) Introduced apply_operation_id and deterministic apply keying. The runtime now consumes ApplyOptimizationConfig(scope, version, apply_operation_id, reason) and records a durable ApplyRecord per (scope, version). If the same apply_ope...
